title: the-loop configuration
description: Schema for .the-loop/config.yaml. A subset of these keys may be overridden per work-item via the YAML front-matter of the work-item / spec markdown.

autonomyPolicy.description: What a risk tier requires before completion: finish autonomously after the review loop, or require a human to approve the PR (and optionally the spec).

x-onboarding.description: Drives the guided onboarding `/the-loop:init` runs (see the the-loop skill's reference/onboarding.md). Groups club related top-level keys that interact and are established with the user together, in this order. Per-key explanations, defaults, enum possibilities and examples come from the property schemas below — this block only adds grouping and ask policy, so the schema stays the single source of truth. `version` belongs to no group: init stamps it. Validators ignore x-* keywords.

askLevels.always: No sensible default exists — init MUST establish this group with the user (or report it under needs-user when running non-interactively).
askLevels.confirm: Init proposes sensible values (detected from the repo, else the schema default) and asks the user to confirm or adjust the whole group in one pass.
askLevels.advanced: Sensible defaults are applied silently; the group is only walked through when the user asks for the full tour.

groups.title: Project & ticketing
groups.explain: Where work items (tickets) live and how the repository is laid out. Every work item needs a ticket, so the loop cannot run until this is right.

groups.title: Languages & tooling
groups.explain: The exact package manager / test runner / linter / type-checker per language. Detected from lock files, manifests and CI config; CI must use exactly the same tooling as local.

groups.title: Workflow & phases
groups.explain: The Kiro-style 3-phase spec workflow (requirements → design → tasks), where specs and capability docs live, and the phase labels tracked on the ticket.

groups.title: Quality gates
groups.explain: What runs before a commit/push (lint, typecheck, tests), the commit convention, the TDD discipline, Gherkin scenario docstrings on integration tests, and the minimalism guard.

groups.title: Reviews, security & autonomy
groups.explain: How many self/critic review rounds run before escalating to a human, the security gates woven into the phases (threat model, security design, security review, human sign-off tier), and how much a work item may complete without a human (risk tiers 1-Trivial … 5-Critical).

groups.title: People & communication
groups.explain: Who approves work (personas/roles — every decision needs a paper trail), optional notification channels, and how the harness presents work to and educates humans.

groups.title: API contracts & design artifacts
groups.explain: Contract-first API specs (OpenAPI/GraphQL) and how UI/UX design artifacts are captured in the design phase. Only relevant to projects exposing an API or a user-facing surface.

groups.title: Observability & local orchestration
groups.explain: Logging levels (dev == runtime, only breakpoints differ), browser logging, container runtime and local service wiring for multi-artifact testing, and the external-tools registry.

groups.title: Automation ingress & self-improvement
groups.explain: The webhook receiver / poller that route GitHub events into harness sessions, and the learnings lifecycle. Localhost/off by default; configure only when running the-loop's runtime automation.

version.description: Schema version of this config file. Used by /upgrade-the-loop to reconcile.

owner.description: GitHub org/user that owns the repository.

projectKey.description: Jira project key work items are created under.

monorepoTool.description: Only meaningful when monorepo is true. Default is Nx; the-loop must also work when monorepo is false.

runScriptsFromRoot.description: All scripts are invoked from the project root.

workflow.description: The Kiro-style 3-phase spec workflow and the phase state machine tracked on the ticket.

specApproach.description: (optional brainstorm.md ->) requirements(.md)/bugfix(.md) -> design.md -> tasks.md, each artifact iterated with feedback and gated by a human review before advancing.

requireHumanReviewPerPhase.description: A human review/approval is required at the end of each spec phase.

capabilitiesDir.description: Living capability docs (the organized view of specs; single source of truth for a capability's current behaviour) live in <capabilitiesDir>/, indexed by <capabilitiesDir>/capabilities.md. Affected docs are updated in the same PR as the work item (ready-to-ship gate item).

phaseLabelPrefix.description: Prefix for the labels/tags created in the ticketing system to track phase.

phases.description: Ordered phase state machine for a work item. Each maps to a ticket label. `brainstorming` is optional — a work item may enter it or start directly at `requirements-definition`.

tooling.description: Per-language tooling. RULE: CI/CD must use exactly the same tooling as local.

lint.description: RULE: all files including markdown should be linted.

testing.description: Integration-test scenario conventions and how the harness queries them. See reference/testing.md.

gherkinDocstrings.description: RULE: every integration test carries a Gherkin-syntax docstring (Feature/Scenario/Given-When-Then) naming the scenario under test.

linkRequirements.description: When a test is tied to a requirements.md, its docstring includes a `Requirement:` link to that file (and section anchor).

integrationTestGlobs.description: Globs the harness scans to enumerate integration-test scenarios (`the-loop scenarios`). Empty = use the CLI's built-in defaults.

apiSpecs.description: Where API contracts are authored and how docs are generated from them. See reference/testing.md (API specs).

dir.description: RULE: all RESTful API specs are authored here in the OpenAPI format.

generateDocs.description: Documentation is generated automatically from the OpenAPI specs.

docsTool.description: Tool that renders docs from OpenAPI. Free-form; e.g. redocly, widdershins, swagger-ui.

graphql.description: GraphQL best practice: an SDL-first schema, checked in, linted/snapshot-tested, and documented.

dir.description: Where the checked-in GraphQL schema lives.

schemaFormat.description: Schema Definition Language — the human-authored, checked-in, snapshot-tested contract.

generateDocs.description: Documentation is generated automatically from the SDL schema.

docsTool.description: Tool that renders docs from the SDL. Free-form; e.g. spectaql, graphdoc, magidoc.

design.description: How UI/UX (visual) design is captured and iterated in the design phase, alongside design.md's architecture/HLD/LLD. See reference/design-artifacts.md.

uiArtifacts.description: Visual design artifacts for work items with a user-facing surface. First-class, tracked design-phase artifacts (siblings of design.md), iterated-until-locked with the designer persona and referenced from the ticket. Work items with no UI simply produce none.

dir.description: Directory (relative to the work item's spec dir, <specDir>/<id>/) holding checked-in UI/UX design artifacts, e.g. docs/specs/<id>/design/.

format.description: Preferred representation for generated visual design: self-contained HTML+CSS+JS prototypes (Claude-artifact style), a linked Figma file, or both.

selfContained.description: RULE for HTML prototypes: self-contained (inline CSS/JS, data-URI assets, no external network deps) so they render standalone in a browser and as a Claude-style artifact.

screenshotEvidence.description: Capture rendered screenshots of the locked design artifacts as design-phase evidence, embedded in the reviewer briefing.

remoteServices.description: Services that should point at a remote instead of running locally.

hooks.description: Pre-commit / pre-push gating. RULE: same tooling as CI.

commitConvention.description: Commit-message convention enforced via a commit-msg hook. RULE: all commits are Conventional Commits (https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/).

observability.description: Logging must be identical at dev-time and run-time; only breakpoints differ.

browserLogging.description: How browser logs are surfaced to the harness, e.g. chrome-devtools-mcp.

eventLog.description: Structured JSONL event log of every routing/dispatch/session decision made by the-loop's CLI processes (gh-webhook, poll, sessions). Queried with `the-loop events`; schema in skills/the-loop/reference/observability.md (decision-025).

reviews.description: Self-review and critic-review loop before escalating to a human. The counts are caps; see reference/reviewing.md for the procedure they drive.

selfReviewCount.description: Max self-review rounds (a cap; the loop stops early when a round yields zero new actionable findings).

criticReviewCount.description: Max critic-review rounds (a cap; also stops early on zero new findings).

stopOnNoNewFindings.description: Stop the review loop as soon as a round surfaces no new actionable finding.

escalateOnRepeatFinding.description: Diminishing-returns guard: if two consecutive rounds surface the SAME finding, escalate to a human instead of looping.

critics.description: Alternate harness/model combos used as critics, e.g. cursor+gpt-5.5 reviewing claude-opus output.

harness.description: Critic harness/CLI. Free-form so any harness works; known examples: claude, cursor, aider, continue, a local model runner.

command.description: Optional explicit invocation for an arbitrary critic harness (how to run it).

autonomy.description: Risk-tiered autonomy: how much a work item may complete without a human, by risk tier (1-Trivial … 5-Critical). See reference/workflow.md.

defaultTier.description: Risk tier assumed when a work item does not declare `riskTier` in its front-matter.

inferFromChange.description: Raise the effective tier automatically when the change touches sensitive areas (auth/security/schema/public API).

sensitivePaths.description: Globs that force a high tier when touched.

tiers.description: Per-tier gate policy (keys "1"–"5"). Lower tiers may complete autonomously after the review loop; higher tiers require the named human gate.

security.description: Security as a first-class, gated concern woven into the phase gates (requirements → design → review), not a separate step. See reference/security.md.

threatModel.description: The threat-model-lite captured in the requirements phase (the Security considerations section of requirements.md / bugfix.md).

required.description: Requirements/bugfix MUST carry a filled Security considerations section (untrusted actors, trust boundaries, abuse cases, fail-closed expectations) to pass the phase gate. "No new attack surface" is a valid answer when written and justified.

projectDoc.description: Optional path to a living project-level threat-model doc the per-work-item sections link to and delta against. Empty = none; the per-work-item sections are the record.

design.description: The Security design section of design.md: how each requirements-phase trust boundary is enforced (authn/authz, input validation, secrets, least privilege, injection surfaces, fail-closed).

required.description: design.md MUST state how every trust boundary from the requirements is enforced; a boundary left unenforced fails the design gate.

review.description: The security review — an explicit ready-to-ship gate item run after the self/critic rounds, before a work item can be marked complete.

required.description: The ready-to-ship gate includes a passed security review; an unresolved security finding blocks completion regardless of risk tier.

mechanism.description: auto = use the harness's built-in security-review skill when available, else the-loop's checklist in reference/security.md; skill / checklist force one mechanism.

humanSignOffMinTier.description: Effective risk tier at or above which a NAMED HUMAN must approve the security review (paper trail) — autonomous completion is off the table even if autonomy.tiers allows it. Below the threshold the autonomous review suffices. 6 = never require a human sign-off.

tdd.description: Test-driven-development discipline in the implementation phase. See reference/workflow.md.

mode.description: standard = red→green→refactor per task; tdd-first = all tests for the work item written and failing before any production code; off = no enforced test-first.

minimalism.description: Generation-time minimalism pass to counter code bloat. Advisory: informs generation, never gates a merge and never trades away validation/error-handling/security/accessibility. See reference/minimalism.md.

selfImprovement.description: Lifecycle for learnings: capture → write-gate → consolidate → inject. See reference/automation.md.

maxIndexLines.description: Cap on the injected learnings index; overflow detail spills to learnings/topics/<category>.md, read on demand.

writeGateOccurrences.description: A learning candidate becomes durable only after recurring this many times (rule-of-three); a clearly high-severity one-off may bypass the gate.

userInteraction.description: How the-loop communicates with humans when requesting input and presenting work. RULES: always give enough context to make the call; all diagrams are mermaid; educating the user on low-level decisions is mandatory, not optional.

diagramFormat.description: RULE: all diagrams (PR summaries, design docs, education) are authored in mermaid.

prSummary.description: What every PR the-loop raises must contain so a human can review AI-authored work efficiently.

required.description: The reviewer briefing MUST be posted/updated in the PR (description or top comment) BEFORE requesting human review — a ready-to-ship gate item, so R10 education is triggered rather than silently skipped.

templatePath.description: Template the reviewer briefing is produced from. the-loop's internal template, resolved under ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} (the installed plugin). Set a repo-relative path to override with your own.

condensed.description: Present a condensed, prioritized summary telling the reviewer where to focus and in what order.

includeDiagrams.description: Include mermaid diagrams explaining the low-level details of the change.

documentInsightsAndDecisions.description: Document the insights from spec->implementation and every low-level decision the harness had to make, in the PR description.

educateUser.description: RULE: intentionally and mandatorily educate the user on low-level design decisions as their familiarity with the codebase decreases. Not optional.

personas.description: People and groups available to collaborate. A user can hold multiple roles. RULE: every decision needs a paper trail, so at least one approver must be defined.

handle.description: GitHub handle or group, e.g. @octocat or @org/team.

messaging.description: Optional channels for notifying/escalating to humans when a decision is pending.

externalTools.description: Tools/skills/MCPs/plugins the-loop should be aware of. Free-form, fully user-configurable.

referenceFile.description: Path to a free-form markdown file describing available tools/MCPs/plugins.

webhooks.description: Config for the the-loop CLI webhook receiver(s) (e.g. `the-loop gh-webhook start`).

secretEnv.description: Env var holding the GitHub webhook secret for HMAC verification (never stored in config).

events.description: GitHub event names of interest, e.g. pull_request_review_comment, workflow_run. Empty = accept all event types.

routing.description: Route received events to registered harness sessions via the session registry (docs/specs/issue-15/design.md, decision-016).

registryDir.description: Directory of per-session registry JSON files (git-ignored).

defaultHarness.description: Harness used when spawning a session for an unmatched event.

spawnOnUnmatched.description: Policy for events matching no registered session: 'never' log-and-drop; 'always' spawn+register a new session; 'labeled' spawn only when the issue/PR carries autoExecuteLabel.

autoExecuteLabel.description: Issue/PR label that opts a work item into autonomous execution (spawnOnUnmatched: labeled). Read from the webhook payload, no API call.

authorizedUsers.description: SECURITY (prompt-injection guard): GitHub logins whose actions the-loop may act on. Comments/reviews/labels/items authored by anyone not listed are ignored by BOTH the webhook receiver and the poller. Empty = fall back to ticketing.github.owner; if that is also unset, human-authored events are all ignored (fail closed). Each operator runs their own instance for their own logins.

spawnWorkdir.description: Working directory for sessions spawned on unmatched events.

runner.description: How spawned sessions are hosted (receiver-global): 'process' = headless one-shot subprocess; 'tmux' = interactive harness TUI in a named tmux session (loop-<slug>) humans can attach to (issue-32, decision-021).

webTerminal.description: Optional ttyd-served browser terminal onto the tmux sessions (runner: tmux). No auth of its own — access control is environmental: localhost, VPN, or the hosting provider's network (decision-021).

enabled.description: Serve tmux sessions over HTTP via ttyd (verified at receiver start).

host.description: Interface/IP ttyd binds. Keep 127.0.0.1 unless the network layer protects wider exposure.

maxConcurrentDispatches.description: Cap on harness dispatches running in parallel (per-session dispatch is always serialized).

dedupCacheSize.description: Bounded LRU of X-GitHub-Delivery ids for at-most-once processing.

dispatchTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for a single harness resume/spawn subprocess.

promptTemplate.description: string.Template rendered into the prompt that resumes a session. the-loop's internal template; the dispatcher falls back to a built-in default when the path is absent (the case in a project repo, which does not carry the-loop's templates). Set a repo-relative path to override.

spawnPromptTemplate.description: string.Template rendered into the prompt for a newly spawned (auto-execute) session — kicks off the-loop's work-on flow. the-loop's internal template; the dispatcher falls back to a built-in default when the path is absent. Set a repo-relative path to override.

harnessArgs.description: Extra CLI args per harness (e.g. permission flags). The dispatcher never widens permissions itself.

polling.description: Config for the the-loop CLI poller (`the-loop poll start`) — a pull-based, provider-agnostic alternative to the webhook receiver for hosts a webhook cannot reach. Each entry in `sources` selects a provider (e.g. github) the system interfaces with; the core carries no provider-specific knobs. Dispatch behaviour (harness, runner, spawn policy, templates, registry) is reused from `webhooks.ghWebhook.routing`.

intervalSeconds.description: Seconds between poll cycles (applies to all sources).

stateFile.description: Durable JSON tracking which comments each item has processed (cross-poll/restart dedup). Git-ignored runtime state.

sources.description: Ordered list of provider-specific poll sources. Each entry MUST name a `provider`; the remaining keys are that provider's own config (unknown to the core).

provider.description: Which poll provider handles this source. Only `github` ships today (jira is reserved for a follow-up).

label.description: Label gating what this source polls. Empty = reuse webhooks.ghWebhook.routing.autoExecuteLabel.

repos.description: [github] Repositories to poll as OWNER/REPO. Empty = fall back to ticketing.github.

ghBinary.description: [github] Path/name of the gh CLI (uses the user's existing gh auth).
